questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements: All keys are locks. All locks are bangles. All bangles are cars. [Dena Bank (PO) 2010]
Conclusions: I. Some cars are locks. II. Some bangles are keys. III. Some cars are keys.

choices.e: All I, II and III follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements: All fruits are leaves. Some leaves are trees. No tree is house. [Dena Bank (PO) 2010]
Conclusions: I. Some houses are fruits. II. Some trees are fruits. III. No house is a fruit.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements: All tables are mirrors. Some mirrors are chairs. All chairs are glasses. [Dena Bank (PO) 2010]
Conclusions: I. Some glasses are mirrors. II. Some chairs are tables. III. Some mirrors are tables.

choices.d: All I, II and III follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements: All calculators are boxes. All boxes are taps. Some taps are machines. [Dena Bank (PO) 2010]
Conclusions: I. Some machines are boxes. II. Some taps are calculators. III. Some boxes are calculators.

choices.d: All I, II and III follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements: Some books are papers. Some papers are desks. Some desks are chairs. [UCO Bank (PO) 2008]
Conclusions: I. Some books are desks. II. Some papers are chairs. III. Some books are chairs.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements: Some pots are buckets. All buckets are tubs. All tubs are drums. [UCO Bank (PO) 2008]
Conclusions: I. Some drums are pots. II. All tubs are buckets. III. Some drums are buckets.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements: All pins are bags. All chalks are bags. All needles are bags. [UCO Bank (PO) 2008]
Conclusions: I. Some needles are pins. II. Some chalks are needles. III. No needle is pin.

choices.d: Either I or III and II follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements: Some buses are trucks. Some trucks are boats. No boat is jeep. [UCO Bank (PO) 2008]
Conclusions: I. Some jeeps are buses. II. Some boats are buses. III. Some jeeps are trucks.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements: All flowers are trees. All trees are jungles. No jungle is hill. [Andhra Bank (PO) 2008]
Conclusions: I. No flower is hill. II. No tree is hill. III. Some jungles are flowers.

choices.e: All I, II and III follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements: Some uniforms are covers. All covers are papers. All papers are bags. [CMAT 2013]
Conclusions: I. All covers are bags. II. Some bags are covers, papers and uniforms. III. Some uniforms are not papers.

choices.d: All I, II and III follow
